$comment: Asset manifest for the Delphi-OBD design-time package.
Each entry produces one PNG under assets/designtime/. The
generator script (generate.py) calls OpenAI's image API with
model 'gpt-image-2' and writes the result to <out>.
Conventions:
- 'kind' is informational; the script doesn't branch on it.
- 'size' is whatever gpt-image-2 supports natively (square
sizes only). The script downscales to 'final_size' with
Lanczos resampling. Component icons end at 16/24/32 px;
asking the model for that small produces mush, so we
generate at 1024 and downscale.
- 'transparent_background' = true makes the script add the
'background: transparent' parameter (gpt-image-2 supports
it natively for PNG). Set to false + put 'pure white
background' in the prompt when you want to chroma-key
later in a graphics tool.
- 'force' on the CLI re-runs even if 'out' exists.
Style guide for prompts (keep consistent across icons):
- Modern 2D vector look, thick clean strokes, high contrast.
- Brand palette only: charcoal black, metallic silver,
vivid orange, white background. No blues, no reds, no
greens, no other accents.
- Let the model decide which colour goes where; the
reference logo is the canonical guide.
- Subtle metallic gradients are allowed on LARGE shapes
(body, cable, frame) when they match the reference
logo. SMALL details — pins, dots, text-like marks,
indicators — must be solid flat colour. Gradients on
small details mush at 16/24 px after downscaling.
- No cartoonish 3D, no bevel, no glow, no drop shadows.
- Centered subject, even padding, no text.

assets.prompt: Modern 2D vector icon for a vehicle diagnostics software component named 'TOBDConnection'. The icon depicts a stylised OBD-II 16-pin trapezoidal connector seen face-on, with a short cable curling out the back. Match the visual style and exact colour palette of the attached reference logo: charcoal black, metallic silver, vivid orange. Subtle metallic gradients are welcome on LARGE shapes (the connector body, the cable) when they match the reference logo. The pins must be SOLID flat colour, no gradients — they will be downscaled to ~16 pixels and gradients on small details mush. Absolutely no other colours — no blue, no red, no green. Pure white background. Centered composition, generous padding, bold even strokes. No text, no drop shadows, no glow, no cartoon 3D, no bevel. Clean enough to read at 16 by 16 pixels after downscaling.
